purpose: This policy applies when <<LAB_NAME>> operates as a blood collection establishment, governing donor recruitment, eligibility screening, consent, and pre-donation testing. Required by 21 CFR 630 (donor requirements) and 21 CFR 606.100 SOPs. Labs that only receive components from suppliers do not apply this policy.

cfr_text_blocks.label: FDA: General donor eligibility requirements
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: A donor is eligible to donate blood or a blood component if you determine and document that the donor is in good health and free from transfusion-transmitted infections as required under this subpart, and meets all other applicable requirements under this subpart.

scope: Applies only when <<LAB_NAME>> operates a blood collection establishment. Personnel involved in donor recruitment, screening, collection, and post-donation follow-up.
policy_statements: Donor eligibility is determined per 21 CFR 630.10 at every donation: in good health, free of transfusion-transmitted infections (per screening questions and required testing), meets demographic and physical criteria, has not received deferral-triggering exposures or treatments.
Donor consent is obtained in writing before donation; the consent covers the donation process, risks, post-donation testing, and notification of abnormal findings.
Donor screening uses the current FDA-recognized Donor History Questionnaire; deferrals are applied per the published criteria and documented.
Post-donation testing for ABO/Rh, antibody screen, and transfusion-transmitted infectious diseases is performed on every collection per 21 CFR 610.40-45.
Donor notification of abnormal findings is performed per 21 CFR 630.40 with the required information and follow-up resources.
Records are retained for at least 10 years per 21 CFR 606.160 and 42 CFR 493.1105(a)(7).
procedure_steps: Donor presents; identity is verified; consent is obtained; the DHQ is administered.
Eligibility is determined per the DHQ responses, physical exam findings, and current deferrals; ineligible donors are deferred with documented reason and reentry criteria.
Collection is performed per the FDA-validated procedure.
Post-collection testing is completed; results are reviewed; abnormal findings trigger donor notification per 21 CFR 630.40.
All steps are documented and retained per the 10-year rule.
definitions: Standardized questionnaire used to screen blood donors for eligibility. FDA-recognized versions are updated periodically.
Temporary or permanent exclusion of a donor from blood donation based on eligibility criteria. Documented with reason and reentry criteria.
